meme_captions: Me: I'll just rest for 5 minutes. Also me: *wakes up 3 hours later*
My diet starts Monday. Also me on Monday: My diet starts next Monday.
Telling myself 'one more episode' for the 7th time tonight.
When the alarm goes off and you remember it's actually your day off.
My brain at 2am: Remember that embarrassing thing from 10 years ago? Let's discuss.
Payday: *arrives* Me and my wallet: We're rich! Three days later: Broke again.
Me: I'm going to sleep early tonight. My bed at 11pm: LOL okay.
My stomach: I'm hungry. My brain: We just ate. My stomach: We did not agree to this.
When you finally clean your room and trip over nothing in the dark anyway.
Me: I'll do it tomorrow. Tomorrow me: I'll do it tomorrow.
The face you make when someone says 'just a quick meeting' and it's 2 hours long.
Telling myself I'll nap for 20 minutes and waking up as a new person in a new era.
My to-do list: 47 tasks. Me: Organizes the list instead of doing the tasks.
When you're at the restaurant and you pick the wrong thing off the menu.
Checking your phone immediately after putting it down like something changed.
My confidence level: Answering questions in my head during the meeting vs. out loud.
Me saving 20% on groceries then spending it on snacks I didn't need.
When someone says 'let's keep it simple' and then explains for 30 minutes.
My weekend plan vs. my actual weekend: Sleep, snack, repeat.
Me ordering a salad: I'm so healthy. Me adding extra cheese and croutons: Balance.
Buying something on sale you didn't need because 'it was too good to pass up.'
When the Wi-Fi goes down and you have to talk to your family like it's 1995.
Me to me: Stop overthinking. Also me: But what if I analyze it just one more time?
The five stages of Mondays: Denial, anger, bargaining, more sleep, finally getting up.
Walking into work on a Monday like I didn't just have two days off.
My wallet crying when I enter any store I didn't plan to go into.
Exercise goal: Run 5km. Reality: Walk to the fridge and back. Still counts.
Me: I'll just check social media for 5 minutes. Three hours later: Where did I go?
Nobody: My brain at 3am: Did I lock the door? Let's check 17 times.
Watching one cooking video then walking into the kitchen like a professional chef.
Pretending I know what I'm doing in life while googling everything.
Me finishing a bag of chips I wasn't even hungry for: We move.
Brain: You should sleep. Also brain: Let me replay every awkward moment from 2014.
The look of confidence I put on every Monday pretending this week will be different.
My face when someone says 'You look tired' — thanks, I did not consent to that feedback.
Being productive for 20 minutes then taking a 2-hour victory nap.
Me buying gym clothes instead of actually going to the gym. Same energy.
Going to bed at 9pm feeling like a champion. Waking up at 5am confused.
That feeling when you rehearsed the whole conversation but it went a completely different way.
Meeting someone new: I'm very calm and collected. My brain: Say something weird. NOW.
When you say yes to plans two weeks out thinking Future You will cancel them.
My anxiety making up problems that don't exist since before I could walk.
Trying to adult: Step 1. What is step 2? Nobody told me about step 2.
